text.title: claude-tool-use Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for claude-tool-use as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Tool schemas with more than 3 levels of nesting.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Deep nesting increases tool-call errors. Keep input schemas flat (2 levels max) for highest reliability.

failure-mode.description: Agents that append only the assistant message (not `response.content` which includes tool_use blocks) break the conversation history format and get API errors.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Tool use blocks must be preserved in message history. Append the full response.content list.

failure-mode.description: `response.content` is a list of mixed `TextBlock` and `ToolUseBlock` objects — iterate and type-check, never index blindly.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Content blocks are polymorphic. Always check type before accessing fields.

failure-mode.description: An infinite tool loop (Claude keeps calling the same tool) indicates a broken tool implementation that returns the same unhelpful result — implement result caching to detect and break cycles.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Set a max-iterations guard. If the guard is reached, break the loop rather than running forever.

failure-mode.description: Tool schemas with `additionalProperties: false` can cause Claude to fail on edge cases; allow additional properties unless strict validation is needed.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Overly strict schemas cause tool use failures. Allow flexibility unless you have a specific reason not to.

failure-mode.description: MCP servers communicate via stdio by default — spawning them as subprocesses requires the agent to manage their lifecycle (start, health check, shutdown).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: For programmatic use, prefer standard tool use over MCP. MCP is optimized for Claude Desktop.

failure-mode.description: Extended Thinking + tool use: thinking blocks appear in `response.content` before tool_use blocks; filter them out when building tool_result messages.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Tool result messages should only contain tool_result blocks, not thinking blocks. Filter appropriately.
